Client Notes:

Client would like a very detail-oriented cleaning every visit. Please pay close attention to corners, baseboards, trim, windows, and blinds, as these are important areas for her and should be cleaned each time.

Master Bedroom

  • NO bedsheet changes, just make the bed
  • Regular dusting and spot cleaning.
  • High and low dusting.

Additional bedrooms

  • Office: just need quick dust and vacuum, in Sept we won't need to do
  • Guest Room: doesn't get used often quick dust check and vacuum.

Bathrooms (2)

  • Deep clean both bathrooms every visit.
  • Please pay special attention to corners, edges, and detailed areas.
  • Client uses a wheelchair, so it is difficult for her to clean these areas herself. Attention to detail in the bathrooms is especially important.
  • toilet has raiser which can be unplugged and moved out of the way.
  • doesn't use bath rugs since it gets caught in the wheelchair, so towels on the floor just need to be shaken and folded and placed on the side.

Kitchen

  • Focus on detailed cleaning, not just surface cleaning.
  • Remember to check Kitchen window
  • Garbage is in one of the closets in the kitchen need to wipe the ledge.

Studio 

  • Do your best to dust thoroughly, as there are a lot of items in this area.
  • Focus on corners, baseboards, and detailed dusting around furniture and belongings.
  • French doors need spot checking (there's two sets)
  • there's additional microwave and air fryer that need to be wiped down.

Living Room

  • Fireplace may have a lot of wood chips etc but just try your best. if possible dust off fireplace

Laundry Room

  • dust as much as possible but don't need to fold clothes at all

|

They don't need all areas of the home cleaned, just the bathrooms, main living areas and kitchen. Used online 3D floorplan of their home from developer ~3000sqft of the cleaning space they are asking 

The wife works at home and will be the main contact and will do a walk through.

tish.notes:
|

During the summer, please knock on the kids' door and make sure the room is cleaned. 

Monday Cleanings - PREMIUM -

•  Sanitize all kitchen surfaces and appliances

•  Sanitize all bathrooms,  change bath rugs if necessary according to last date changed - master spot check for mold if so use bestbet cream cleanser

•  Sweep and mop all floors

•  Vacuum carpeted areas/rugs

•  Empty garbage cans and replace liners

•  NO bed linen change just make all beds - pull tight duvet and bed sheets.

•  Washing one sink load of dishes

•  Rotate cleaning of vertical surfaces such as baseboards, windows, and doors

•  High dusting for ceiling fans, light fixtures, air vents, and cobwebs

•  spot check guest room/bathroom in renovated part of the garage.


Friday Cleanings:

•  Sanitize all kitchen surfaces and appliances

•  Sanitize all bathrooms - change bath rugs if necessary according to last date changed

•  Sweep and mop all floors

•  vacuum carpeted areas/rugs

•  Make all beds

•  Empty garbage cans and replace liners

•  Light horizontal surface dusting

•  Changing of bed linens

•  Master and Mia’s room linens are in the hallways closet next to the laundry basket. Master bedding is first two shelves of bedding, shelf below is Mias

•  Itay - room next to master, bedding is in closet inside the room, top left corner, comforter change located beneath the bed in storage box/bag

•  Jonathan - downstairs room, bedding inside wardrobe on the very left side

Main focus areas weekly: bathrooms, kitchen, butlers counter, high traffic areas (kitchen floor hallways, mud room and entry ways) covered patio furniture/grill (when applicable sweep)

Rotating areas (meaning spot check and do them biweekly or when needed): theater room, laundry room, living room, dining room, entrance sitting room, pantry.

Always make sure kitchen counters are water mark free.

|

Always make sure kitchen counters are water mark free.
